records.ety: [OE. medlen to mix, OF. medler, mesler, F. mler, LL. misculare, a dim. fr. L. miscere to mix. √271. See Mix, and cf. Medley, Mellay.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Meddled (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Meddling (?).]

quotes: More to know Did never meddle with my thoughts.

defs: To interest or engage one's self; to have to do; -- in a good sense.
quotes: Study to be quiet, and to meddle with your own business.

defs: To interest or engage one's self unnecessarily or impertinently, to interfere or busy one's self improperly with another's affairs; specifically, to handle or disturb another's property without permission; -- often followed by with or in.
quotes: Why shouldst thou meddle to thy hurt?
The civil lawyers . . . have meddled in a matter that belongs not to them.
